title: The Last Signal
description: A lone astronaut drifts through a dying space station.
author: Claude

start.content: You wake up to red emergency lights. The station groans around you. Your oxygen reads 42%. A crackling radio sits on the desk. The airlock is to your left.

radio.content: Static. Then a voice: 'If anyone is alive on Kepler-9... the rescue pod is docked at Bay 3. Hurry.' The signal cuts out. Bay 3 is through the engine room.

airlock.content: The airlock control panel flickers. One suit hangs on the wall, visor cracked. Outside is nothing but stars and silence.

engineRoom.content: Pipes hiss steam. A collapsed beam blocks the path to Bay 3. You could try to crawl under it, or reroute through the flooded biology lab.

crawl.content: You squeeze through � almost. The beam shifts and pins your leg. After a long struggle you wrench free, suit torn, hands shaking. Oxygen at 21%.

crawl_rest.content: You close your eyes for just a second. The station shudders. Oxygen at 14%. No more time to rest.

options.displayText: Get up and run to Bay 3

bioLab.content: Knee-deep water, cold as space. Specimen jars float past you. But the path is clear and you reach the far door quickly. Oxygen at 28%.

bay3.content: The pod is there. It is real. A handwritten note is taped to the hatch: 'Auto-launches in 60 seconds. No override.' You strap in and close your eyes.

escape.content: The pod rockets away. Through the small porthole you watch Kepler-9 collapse silently into itself. You made it. You are the last signal.

death_suit.content: The cracked visor holds for three seconds. Then the cold gets in. Your last thought is how beautiful the stars are.
